Title: A NAC triad modulates plant immunity by negatively regulating N-hydroxy pipecolic acid biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AGD2-like defense response protein; aminotransferase-like enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immunity; systemic acquired resistance; defense activation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts L-lysine to 2,3-dehydropipecolic acid in the first committed steps of NHP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established in this paper; transcriptionally repressed by NAC triad
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic pathway engineering; qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR for NAC90 binding; dual-luciferase repression assay; metabolite profiling; pathogen infection assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALD1 is one of the three Arabidopsis NHP biosynthetic genes used to engineer constitutive NHP overproduction. 2. Its transcript is elevated in nac90 and nac36 mutants and reduced in NAC90 overexpression lines. 3. NAC90 directly binds the ALD1 promoter and represses its expression. 4. ALD1 is part of the NAC triad-repressed module controlling NHP accumulation and immunity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes NHP biosynthesis and immune activation when expressed; repression lowers NHP-associated defense outputs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by pathogen infection; elevated in nac90 and nac36 mutants; reduced in NAC90 overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola ES4326 infection; NHP overproduction background
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive pathway activation is associated with small rosette size and early senescence in pNHP plants
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering enhanced disease resistance, but likely requires balancing with negative regulators to avoid growth penalties
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NAC90; NAC61; NAC36 (transcriptional repressors of promoter expression)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: In transgenic pNHP plants, Arabidopsis pathway genes were constitutively expressed (specific promoter for pAtNHP vector not stated here)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used both as endogenous gene and as transgene in Arabidopsis and tomato pathway engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immunity; systemic acquired resistance; defense activation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 2,3-dehydropipecolic acid to pipecolic acid in the NHP biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic pathway engineering; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase repression assay; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SARD4 is one of the three Arabidopsis genes sufficient with ALD1 and FMO1 to engineer constitutive NHP production. 2. Its transcript is elevated in nac90 and nac36 mutants and reduced in NAC90 overexpression lines. 3. NAC90 represses SARD4 promoter activity in dual-luciferase assays, although ChIP support for direct binding was not statistically significant. 4. SARD4 participates in the NAC triad-regulated NHP biosynthetic module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports Pip and downstream NHP accumulation; transcriptional repression reduces NHP pathway output
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by pathogen infection; elevated in nac90 and nac36 mutants; reduced in NAC90 overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola ES4326 infection; NHP overproduction background
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive pathway activation contributes to defense-growth tradeoff in pNHP plants
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with ALD1 and FMO1 for NHP engineering, but requires control to limit growth penalties
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NAC90; NAC61; NAC36 (transcriptional repressors of promoter expression)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: In transgenic pNHP/tpNHP plants, Arabidopsis pathway genes were constitutively expressed (specific promoter not stated here)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NAC90 showed only a trend, not significant direct promoter binding by ChIP-qPCR in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immunity; systemic acquired resistance; defense activation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates pipecolic acid to form N-hydroxy pipecolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant supported by genetics in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic pathway engineering; knockout/double mutant analysis (fmo1, nac90-1 fmo1); qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR for NAC90 binding; dual-luciferase repression assay; metabolite profiling; pathogen resistance assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FMO1 is essential for NHP biosynthesis and one of the three Arabidopsis genes used to engineer constitutive NHP production in Arabidopsis and tomato. 2. NAC90 directly binds and represses the FMO1 promoter, and FMO1 transcript is elevated in nac90 and nac36 mutants but reduced in NAC90 overexpression lines. 3. The constitutive immune activation, growth defects, and elevated PR1 in nac90 are abolished in the fmo1 background, demonstrating that FMO1-mediated NHP biosynthesis is required for nac90 phenotypes. 4. FMO1 positively affects NHP accumulation and disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly promotes NHP accumulation and immune activation; loss of FMO1 suppresses constitutive defense in nac90
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by pathogen infection; elevated in nac90 and nac36 mutants; reduced in NAC90 overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola ES4326 infection; NHP overproduction background; pathogen challenge
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced FMO1-dependent NHP production associates with dwarfing, early senescence, and growth-defense tradeoff
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate to enhance disease resistance via NHP engineering, but should be paired with regulatory brakes to minimize biomass/yield penalties
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NAC90; NAC61; NAC36 (transcriptional repressors of promoter expression/functionally upstream)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: In transgenic pNHP/tpNHP plants, Arabidopsis pathway genes were constitutively expressed (specific promoter not stated here)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Genetic epistasis with nac90 places FMO1-dependent NHP biosynthesis downstream of NAC90-mediated immune repression.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immunity; defense signaling; systemic acquired resistance
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in the SA biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for SA biosynthesis supported by genetics in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR for NAC90 binding; dual-luciferase repression assay; metabolite profiling; double mutant analysis with sid2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS1 transcript is elevated in nac90 and nac36 mutants and reduced in NAC90 overexpression lines. 2. NAC90 directly binds the ICS1 promoter and represses its expression. 3. Loss of ICS1 function in nac90-1 sid2 suppresses constitutive defense activation and partially rescues dwarf morphology. 4. ICS1-dependent SA biosynthesis contributes to nac90 immune phenotypes together with FMO1-mediated NHP biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA accumulation and contributes to constitutive immune activation in nac90
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by pathogen infection; elevated in nac90 and nac36 mutants; reduced in NAC90 overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola ES4326 infection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher SA associates with early senescence and reduced growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning defense outputs, but requires moderation to avoid growth penalties
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NAC90; NAC61; NAC36 (transcriptional repressors of promoter expression)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter assayed by dual luciferase
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: sid2 is the ICS1 mutant used genetically in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: non-protein amino acid; phytohormone/phenolic defense metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immunity; systemic acquired resistance; growth-defense tradeoff regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Label-free proteomics; T-DNA knockout mutants; overexpression lines; qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase reporter assay; metabolite profiling; pathogen infection assay; genetic epistasis with fmo1 and sid2; co-IP; split luciferase complementation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAC90 protein is strongly induced in NHP-overproducing Arabidopsis and its transcript is induced by pathogen, NHP, and SA. 2. nac90 mutants show constitutive immune activation, early senescence, elevated NHP and SA, and increased expression of ALD1, SARD4, FMO1, and ICS1. 3. NAC90 overexpression lowers NHP and SA accumulation and compromises disease resistance. 4. NAC90 directly represses ALD1, FMO1, and ICS1 and represses SARD4 promoter activity. 5. FMO1 and ICS1 are required for the constitutive defense phenotypes of nac90. 6. NAC90 interacts with NAC61 and NAC36 to form a repressive NAC triad.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of NHP and SA biosynthesis; suppresses immunity and limits growth-defense tradeoff costs
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct negative regulator and feedback brake on NHP/SA production; central repressor identified by this paper
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ALD1; FMO1; ICS1; SARD4 promoter activity; indirectly PR1 and defense programs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of NHP and SA; defense-associated metabolic activation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback: induced by pathogen, NHP, SA, and SARD1, then represses NHP/SA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful brake for tuning disease resistance with reduced growth penalty; manipulation may need balancing because knockout enhances defense but causes dwarfing/senescence
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by NHP overproduction, Psm ES4326 infection, exogenous NHP, and SA; reduced pathogen induction in fmo1, sid2, npr1, eds1, pad4, sard1 backgrounds; elevated in ugt76b1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by pathogen, SA, Pip, and NHP according to this and cited data
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses ALD1, FMO1, ICS1, and SARD4 promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported to repress SA biosynthesis during senescence; here extended to NHP pathway control and immunity
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes small rosette and early senescence but enhanced resistance; overexpression enlarges rosettes but compromises disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics: PXD054472; RNA-seq: PRJNA996953; compared with ArrayExpress E-MTAB-10230
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms heteromeric NAC triad with NAC61 and NAC36
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pathogen infection; NHP; SA; SARD1; FMO1/ICS1/NPR1/EDS1/PAD4-dependent immune signaling; negatively influenced by UGT76B1 activity
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola ES4326 infection; exogenous NHP; exogenous SA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UBQ10pro used for constitutive NAC90 overexpression; native promoter used in reporter assays

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: non-protein amino acid; phytohormone/phenolic defense metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immunity; systemic acquired resistance; growth-defense tradeoff regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 mutants; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling; pathogen infection assays; co-IP; split luciferase complementation; double mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAC36 is co-expressed with NHP biosynthetic genes and induced by pathogen, NHP, and SA. 2. nac36 mutants exhibit reduced growth, early senescence, enhanced resistance to Psm ES4326, and elevated Pip, NHP, NHPG, SA, and SAG. 3. FMO1, ALD1, SARD4, ICS1, and PR1 transcripts are elevated in nac36 mutants. 4. NAC36 interacts with NAC90 and NAC61 and participates in a NAC triad that represses NHP/SA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of NHP and SA accumulation and plant immunity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as one member of the repressive NAC triad controlling NHP/SA biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ALD1; SARD4; FMO1; ICS1; PR1-associated defense output
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of NHP and SA; defense-associated transcriptional output
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely negative feedback because induced by pathogen, NHP, and SA while repressing pathway output
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to elevate defense metabolites, though loss causes growth and senescence penalties
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Psm ES4326, NHP, and SA; upregulated in nac90 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negative effect inferred from mutant transcript increases in ALD1, SARD4, FMO1, and ICS1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression, likely via heterodimeric NAC complexes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct promoter binding was not shown for NAC36 in this paper; role supported genetically and by interaction data
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss enhances resistance but reduces growth and accelerates senescence
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms heterodimers with NAC90 and NAC61
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola ES4326 infection; exogenous NHP; exogenous SA

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: non-protein amino acid; phytohormone/phenolic defense metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immunity; systemic acquired resistance; growth-defense tradeoff regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 mutants; metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; co-IP; split luciferase complementation; double mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAC61 is co-expressed with NHP biosynthetic genes and induced by pathogen, NHP, and SA. 2. Single nac61 mutants show little or no obvious change in morphology, defense metabolite accumulation, or disease resistance. 3. NAC61 physically interacts with NAC90 and NAC36. 4. Double mutant combinations involving nac61 show elevated NHP/SA-related transcripts and enhanced resistance, indicating partial redundancy within the NAC triad.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Context-dependent negative regulator within the NAC triad; minor effect alone but contributes redundantly to suppress NHP/SA-associated immunity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent co-repressor within a heteromeric NAC module rather than a strong standalone regulator
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ALD1; SARD4; FMO1; ICS1-associated module inferred from double mutant phenotypes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of NHP and SA; defense repression in concert with NAC90/NAC36
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely negative feedback because it is induced by pathogen, NHP, and SA while functioning in the repressive triad
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be combined with other NAC regulators for fine-tuning defense, but standalone manipulation may have limited effect
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Psm ES4326, NHP, and SA; upregulated in nac90 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negative regulatory contribution inferred from higher pathway gene expression in double mutants
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression via NAC protein complex/heterodimerization
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No strong single-mutant phenotype for NHP/SA accumulation or resistance under the conditions tested
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not clearly established alone; redundancy may buffer tradeoffs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms heterodimers with NAC90 and NAC36
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola ES4326 infection; exogenous NHP; exogenous SA
